frameworks.name: metacognitive decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: Subjective certainty in ambiguous sensory cues modulates choice and learning

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making under uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: Orientation discrimination with spatial stimulus-response rule in rats

frameworks.name: reversal learning
frameworks.one_line: Confidence is shown to enhance subsequent rule reversal

frameworks.name: chemogenetic circuit dissociation
frameworks.one_line: DREADD-based silencing dissociates ACC vs BLA contributions

core_question: Do anterior cingulate cortex and basolateral amygdala play dissociable roles in computing decision confidence and using it to learn under uncertainty?
key_assumptions: Self-timed waiting for reward is a valid proxy for decision confidence
Visual stimulus orientation ambiguity parametrically modulates confidence
Chemogenetic inhibition isolates causal contribution of targeted region
Confidence signals can feed back to gate learning from outcome
key_findings: Waiting times scaled with discrimination accuracy, validating them as a confidence proxy
BLA silencing shortened waiting times overall
ACC inhibition rendered waiting times insensitive to confidence-modulating stimulus attributes
Reversal learning was enhanced by confidence, and both ACC and BLA inhibition blocked this enhancement
interpretations: ACC contributes to confidence computations whereas BLA does not, despite affecting overall waiting
ACC and BLA play dissociable roles in transmitting confidence and shaping learning strategies under uncertainty
